messages.content:
1_ Translate the following legal text into colloquial Farsi 2_ Place the Persian and English text side by side in the table 3_ From the beginning to the end of the text, there should be an English sentence on the left side and a Persian sentence on the right side.
       4- Using legal language for Persian translation

          .4
Masters.
Masters of the Senior Courts are salaried officials, lawyers of at least ten years' standing, attached either to the Queen's Bench Division or to the Chancery Division of the High Court.
Masters of the Queen's Bench Division adjudicate on all matters preliminary to a trial. These preliminaries are known as 'interlocutory matters'. Thus one party may wish to inspect documents in the possession of their opponent, or one party may wish to put questions to their opponent to clarify certain points in issue. In these circumstances application may be made to a master for an order for discovery or an order for interrogatories commanding the oppon- ent to produce the required documents or to answer on oath written questions. Disputes may arise as to the most convenient time or place of trial, or whether the trial should be with or without a jury. Such disputes may be decided by a master, from whose decision appeal lies to a judge in chambers. They are appointed from barristers.
Chancery Division Masters perform similar work in their Division of the High Court. They are appointed from among solicitors.
Taxing Masters are officers of the Senior Courts whose function is the checking, determining, and levying of costs to be paid by parties to the trial when the court so directs.

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|
|Masters.|استادان.|
|Masters of the Senior Courts are salaried officials, lawyers of at least ten years' standing, attached either to the Queen's Bench Division or to the Chancery Division of the High Court.|استادان دادگاه‌های عالی مقامات حقوق‌بگیری هستند، وکلا با حداقل ده سال سابقه، که به بخش نیمکت پادشاهی یا به بخش داوری دادگاه عالی متصلند.|
|Masters of the Queen's Bench Division adjudicate on all matters preliminary to a trial.|استادان بخش نیمکت پادشاهی در مورد تمام مسائل مقدماتی یک محاکمه قضاوت می‌کنند.|
|These preliminaries are known as 'interlocutory matters'.|این مقدمات به عنوان 'مسائل میانجی' شناخته می‌شوند.|
|Thus one party may wish to inspect documents in the possession of their opponent, or one party may wish to put questions to their opponent to clarify certain points in issue.|بنابراین یک طرف ممکن است بخواهد اسنادی را که در اختیار حریفش است، بازرسی کند، یا یک طرف ممکن است بخواهد به حریف خود سوالاتی بپرسد تا نکات خاصی را در مورد مسئله مشخص کند.|
|In these circumstances application may be made to a master for an order for discovery or an order for interrogatories commanding the opponent to produce the required documents or to answer on oath written questions.|در این شرایط، می‌توان درخواستی به یک استاد برای صدور حکم کشف یا حکم بازجویی ارائه داد، که از حریف می‌خواهد اسناد مورد نیاز را تهیه کند یا به سوالات نوشته شده به صورت قسم جواب دهد.|
|Disputes may arise as to the most convenient time or place of trial, or whether the trial should be with or without a jury.|اختلافات ممکن است در مورد مناسب‌ترین زمان یا مکان محاکمه، یا اینکه آیا محاکمه باید با هیئت منصفه یا بدون هیئت منصفه انجام شود، ایجاد شود.|
|Such disputes may be decided by a master, from whose decision appeal lies to a judge in chambers.|چنین اختلافاتی ممکن است توسط یک استاد حل و فصل شود، که تصمیم او قابل تجدید نظر توسط یک قاضی در دفتر خود می‌باشد.|
|They are appointed from barristers.|آنها از بین وکلای دادگستری منصوب می‌شوند.|
|Chancery Division Masters perform similar work in their Division of the High Court.|استادان بخش داوری کار مشابهی را در بخش خودشان از دادگاه عالی انجام می‌دهند.|
|They are appointed from among solicitors.|آنها از بین وکلای مشاور منصوب می‌شوند.|
|Taxing Masters are officers of the Senior Courts whose function is the checking, determining, and levying of costs to be paid by parties to the trial when the court so directs.|استادان مالیاتی مقامات دادگاه‌های عالی هستند که وظیفه‌شان بررسی، تعیین، و وضع هزینه‌هایی است که توسط اطراف دعوی پرداخت می‌شود زمانی که دادگاه چنین دستوری می‌دهد.|

5
Director of
Public
Prosecutions
The office of the Director of Public Prosecutions is governed by the Prosecution of Offences Act, 1985. The Director must be a barrister or solici- tor of at least ten years' standing. This is an official appointment by the Attorney General with responsibility for the Crown Prosecution Service. This service, set up under the 1985 Act, is staffed by barristers and solicitors, certain of whom are designated Crown Prosecutors and Chief Crown Prosecutors (who are responsible for the service in each area in England and Wales).
The Director, in his or her capacity as head of the Service, is responsible for all criminal proceedings on behalf of the police (other than minor criminal offences). He or she must also conduct all binding-over proceedings instigated on behalf of the police force and any other proceedings where, because of the importance, difficulty or otherwise of the case, may be considered necessary. The Crown Prosecution Service Inspectorate Act, 2000 provided for the Attorney General to appoint a Chief Inspector of the Crown Prosecution Service with the power to establish an inspectorate with the primary function of inspecting the operation of the service. (This commenced 1 October 2001.)
88

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|
|Director of Public Prosecutions|مدیریت تعقیبات عمومی|
|The office of the Director of Public Prosecutions is governed by the Prosecution of Offences Act, 1985.|دفتر مدیریت تعقیبات عمومی تحت قانون تعقیب جرائم، ۱۹۸۵ اداره می‌شود.|
|The Director must be a barrister or solicitor of at least ten years' standing.|مدیر باید حداقل ده سال سابقه به عنوان وکیل دادگستری یا وکیل مشاور داشته باشد.|
|This is an official appointment by the Attorney General with responsibility for the Crown Prosecution Service.|این یک منصب رسمی توسط دادستان کل است که مسئولیت خدمات تعقیب کیفری را بر عهده دارد.|
|This service, set up under the 1985 Act, is staffed by barristers and solicitors, certain of whom are designated Crown Prosecutors and Chief Crown Prosecutors (who are responsible for the service in each area in England and Wales).|این خدمت که تحت قانون ۱۹۸۵ تاسیس شده، توسط وکلای دادگستری و وکلای مشاور تشکیل شده است، برخی از آنها به عنوان دادستان‌ها و دادستان‌های ارشد معین شده‌اند (که مسئولیت خدمات را در هر منطقه در انگلیس و ولز بر عهده دارند).|
|The Director, in his or her capacity as head of the Service, is responsible for all criminal proceedings on behalf of the police (other than minor criminal offences).|مدیر، به عنوان رئیس خدمات، مسئول تمامی روندهای کیفری به نمایندگی از پلیس است (به جز جرائم کیفری جزئی).|
|He or she must also conduct all binding-over proceedings instigated on behalf of the police force and any other proceedings where, because of the importance, difficulty or otherwise of the case, may be considered necessary.|او همچنین باید تمام روندهای تعهد به حفظ نظمی که به نمایندگی از نیروی پلیس آغاز شده‌اند و هرگونه روند دیگری را که به دلیل اهمیت، دشواری یا ویژگی‌های دیگر پرونده، ضروری تلقی شود، اجرا کند.|
|The Crown Prosecution Service Inspectorate Act, 2000 provided for the Attorney General to appoint a Chief Inspector of the Crown Prosecution Service with the power to establish an inspectorate with the primary function of inspecting the operation of the service.|قانون بازرسی خدمات تعقیب کیفری، ۲۰۰۰ به دادستان کل اجازه داد تا یک بازرس ارشد خدمات تعقیب کیفری را منصوب کند که قدرت تاسیس یک بازرسی با وظیفه اصلی بازرسی عملیات خدمات را دارد. (این از ۱ اکتبر ۲۰۰۱ آغاز شد.)|

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|
|The legal profession|حرفه حقوقی|
|There are two branches of the legal profession in Britain: (i) barristers and (ii) solicitors of the Supreme Court.|دو شاخه در حرفه حقوقی در بریتانیا وجود دارد: (۱) وکلای دادگستری و (۲) وکلای دیوان عالی.|
|In most other countries, including some parts of the Commonwealth, there is no such division.|در بیشتر کشورهای دیگر، از جمله برخی از بخش‌های کشورهای مشترک‌المنافع، چنین تقسیم‌بندی‌ای وجود ندارد.|
|Solicitors|وکلای مشاور|
|The modern solicitor is the successor of three former ancient professions known as attorney (or representative), solicitor, and proctor.|وکیل مشاور مدرن جانشین سه حرفه قدیمی است که به عنوان وکیل (یا نماینده)، وکیل مشاور، و وکیل عدلیه شناخته می‌شوند.|
|These assisted judges in the King's Bench in the early stages of litigation or carried out the less skilled work in the ecclesiastical and Admiralty courts.|اینها در مراحل اولیه دعاوی به قضات در دادگاه پادشاه کمک می‌کردند یا کارهای کم‌مهارت‌تری را در دادگاه‌های کلیسایی و دادگاه‌های دریایی انجام می‌دادند.|
|By a succession of Solicitors Acts, 1939 to 1974, the profession has been unified and regulated (the 1974 Act consolidates the law).|با یک سلسله از قوانین وکلای مشاور، از ۱۹۳۹ تا ۱۹۷۴، این حرفه واحد و تنظیم شده است (قانون ۱۹۷۴ قانون را مستحکم می‌کند).|
|The regulations of the Law Society provide for: (i) the scales of remuneration and fees of solicitors; (ii) the terms and conditions of articles of clerkship for new entrants; (iii) courses for the education and training of students; (iv) the conduct of examinations; (v) the discipline of all solicitors.|مقررات انجمن حقوقی شامل موارد زیر است: (۱) مقیاس‌های جبران خدمت و هزینه‌های وکلای مشاور؛ (۲) شرایط و ضوابط مقالات کارآموزی برای ورودی‌های جدید؛ (۳) دوره‌های آموزشی و آموزش دانشجویان؛ (۴) رفتار در امتحانات؛ (۵) انضباط تمام وکلای مشاور.|

A person who has either completed a law degree or, if a non-law graduate, has completed the Common Professional Examination, and undertaken a one year Legal Practice Course followed by two terms as a trainee solicitor may be admitted a solicitor by having his or her name enrolled. Enrolled solicitors thereby become officers of the Supreme Court, and each receives a Certificate to Practise which is renewable annually. As from 1 October 1984 solicitors are permitted to advertise on certain terms and also advertise properties for sale. In accordance with the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 they are also permitted to form partnerships with foreign firms.
Duties of a Solicitor. Most solicitors are employed in private practice, either alone or in a partnership firm, with the right to incorporate with limited or unlimited liability. Others are employed in the public service, industry, and commerce.
Practising solicitors are consulted by, and receive instructions from, lay clients on a wide variety of matters both civil and criminal, e.g. the making of wills, administration of estates, family matters, the formation of companies, drawing up of documents, conveyancing, and criminal offences of all kinds. In cases of unusual difficulty or where a trial is to take place in the superior courts, the solicitor takes instructions from the client, prepares a brief and approaches a barrister (counsel) to give an ‘opinion' or represent the client at the trial.
Solicitors' rights of audience in the magistrates' courts, county courts and limited rights of audience in the Crown Court and the Supreme Court have now been extended by the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990.
The relationship between solicitor and client is based on professional confidence, and a solicitor cannot be compelled to disclose in court commu- nications made in a professional relationship. Nor is a solicitor liable for defamation in respect of statements made in court during the course of a trial. Solicitors are, however, liable to be sued for damages for negligence in the conduct of their profession: e.g. where they have carelessly lost docu- ments entrusted to them. In certain situations it has also been held that a solicitor might owe a duty of care in tort to a third party (Ross v. Caunters, 1980 and Al-Kandari v. J.R. Brown & Co., 1987).
89

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|
|A person who has either completed a law degree or, if a non-law graduate, has completed the Common Professional Examination, and undertaken a one year Legal Practice Course followed by two terms as a trainee solicitor may be admitted a solicitor by having his or her name enrolled.|شخصی که یا مدرک حقوق کامل کرده یا اگر فارغ‌التحصیل غیرحقوقی است، امتحان حرفه‌ای عمومی را گذرانده و یک دوره عملی حقوقی یک ساله را دنبال کرده و دو دوره به عنوان وکیل مشاور کارآموز گذرانده است، می‌تواند با ثبت نامش به عنوان وکیل مشاور پذیرفته شود.|
|Enrolled solicitors thereby become officers of the Supreme Court, and each receives a Certificate to Practice which is renewable annually.|وکلاي ثبت‌نام‌شده بدین ترتیب به افسران دیوان عالی تبدیل می‌شوند، و هر کدام سند اجازه تمرین دریافت می‌کنند که سالانه قابل تجدید است.|
|As from 1 October 1984 solicitors are permitted to advertise on certain terms and also advertise properties for sale.|از تاریخ ۱ اکتبر ۱۹۸۴ وکلای مشاور اجازه دارند تحت شرایط خاصی تبلیغ کنند و همچنین ملک‌هایی را برای فروش تبلیغ نمایند.|
|In accordance with the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 they are also permitted to form partnerships with foreign firms.|بر اساس قانون دادگاه‌ها و خدمات حقوقی ۱۹۹۰، آنها همچنین اجازه دارند با شرکت‌های خارجی شراکت تشکیل دهند.|
|Duties of a Solicitor. Most solicitors are employed in private practice, either alone or in a partnership firm, with the right to incorporate with limited or unlimited liability.|وظایف یک وکیل مشاور. بیشتر وکلای مشاور در تمرین خصوصی مشغول به کار هستند، چه به تنهایی و چه در یک شرکت شراکتی، با حق تاسیس شرکت با مسئولیت محدود یا نامحدود.|
|Others are employed in the public service, industry, and commerce.|برخی دیگر در خدمات عمومی، صنعت و تجارت استخدام شده‌اند.|
|Practising solicitors are consulted by, and receive instructions from, lay clients on a wide variety of matters both civil and criminal, e.g. the making of wills, administration of estates, family matters, the formation of companies, drawing up of documents, conveyancing, and criminal offences of all kinds.|وکلای مشاور ممارست در مشورت و دریافت دستورالعمل‌هایی از مراجعین عادی در انواع گسترده‌ای از موضوعات مدنی و کیفری هستند، مانند تنظیم وصیت‌نامه‌ها، مدیریت املاک، مسائل خانوادگی، تشکیل شرکت‌ها، تهیه اسناد، انتقال املاک و جرائم کیفری انواع مختلف.|
|In cases of unusual difficulty or where a trial is to take place in the superior courts, the solicitor takes instructions from the client, prepares a brief and approaches a barrister (counsel) to give an ‘opinion' or represent the client at the trial.|در مواردی که مشکلاتی غیرمعمول وجود دارد یا محاکمه‌ای قرار است در دادگاه‌های عالی انجام شود، وکیل مشاور دستورالعمل‌هایی از موکل دریافت می‌کند، خلاصه‌ای آماده می‌کند و به سمت وکیل دادگستری (مشاور) می‌رود تا نظری ارائه دهد یا در دادگاه موکل را نمایندگی کند.|
|Solicitors' rights of audience in the magistrates' courts, county courts and limited rights of audience in the Crown Court and the Supreme Court have now been extended by the Courts and Legal Services Act 1990.|حقوق حضور وکلای مشاور در دادگاه‌های مجیسترالی، دادگاه‌های شهرستان و حقوق محدود حضور در دادگاه تاج و دیوان عالی اکنون توسط قانون دادگاه‌ها و خدمات حقوقی ۱۹۹۰ گسترش یافته است.|
|The relationship between solicitor and client is based on professional confidence, and a solicitor cannot be compelled to disclose in court communications made in a professional relationship.|رابطه بین وکیل مشاور و موکل بر اساس اعتماد حرفه‌ای است، و یک وکیل مشاور نمی‌تواند مجبور به افشای ارتباطاتی شود که در یک رابطه حرفه‌ای انجام شده است.|
|Nor is a solicitor liable for defamation in respect of statements made in court during the course of a trial.|یک وکیل مشاور همچنین مسئول توهین به شهرت در مورد بیانیه‌هایی که در طول یک محاکمه در دادگاه انجام شده، نیست.|
|Solicitors are, however, liable to be sued for damages for negligence in the conduct of their profession: e.g. where they have carelessly lost documents entrusted to them.|وکلای مشاور، با این حال، ممکن است به دلیل غفلت در انجام وظایف حرفه‌ای خود مورد شکایت برای خسارت قرار گیرند: به عنوان مثال زمانی که آنها بی‌احتیاطی اسناد سپرده شده به آنها را گم کنند.|
|In certain situations it has also been held that a solicitor might owe a duty of care in tort to a third party (Ross v. Caunters, 1980 and Al-Kandari v. J.R. Brown & Co., 1987).|در شرایط خاصی همچنین تصمیم گرفته شده است که یک وکیل مشاور ممکن است مسئولیت مراقبت در قبال ضرر به شخص ثالثی داشته باشد (روس در برابر کائونترز، ۱۹۸۰ و ال‌کنداری در برابر جی.آر. براون و شرکا، ۱۹۸۷).|

7 Barristers
Anyone wishing to become a barrister must join one of the four Inns of Court: Gray's Inn, Lincoln's Inn, Inner Temple, or Middle Temple. These four Inns of Court are unincorporated bodies of medieval origin, owned and controlled by their senior members called the Masters of the Bench.
The Senate of the Inns of Court and the Bar, formed in 1974, can be regarded as the governing body of the Bar today, since the Inns follow the general policy laid down by the Senate and the judges have agreed that discip- linary powers shall be exercised in accordance with the Senate's regulations. Intending barristers must make a certain number of attendances (known as 'keeping term') at their Inn, and to qualify for Call to the Bar they must
The judiciary, law officers and the legal profession 75
either be a law graduate or, if a non-law graduate, have completed the Common Professional Examination. This is followed by a year's Professional Training course before undertaking one year's pupillage in chambers. After six months barristers have the right of audience in any court of law in England and Wales. As noted above, they may take instructions only from a solicitor, not from a lay client direct but unlike the solicitor, they may not sue for their fees.

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|
|Barristers|وکلای دادگستری|
|Anyone wishing to become a barrister must join one of the four Inns of Court: Gray's Inn, Lincoln's Inn, Inner Temple, or Middle Temple.|هر کسی که بخواهد وکیل دادگستری شود باید به یکی از چهار محکمه قضایی بپیوندد: این محکمه‌ها عبارتند از محکمه گری، محکمه لینکلن، معبد داخلی یا معبد میانه.|
|These four Inns of Court are unincorporated bodies of medieval origin, owned and controlled by their senior members called the Masters of the Bench.|این چهار محکمه قضایی نهادهایی غیرمنشوری با منشاء قرون وسطایی هستند که توسط اعضای ارشد خود به نام استادان نیمکت مالکیت و کنترل می‌شوند.|
|The Senate of the Inns of Court and the Bar, formed in 1974, can be regarded as the governing body of the Bar today, since the Inns follow the general policy laid down by the Senate and the judges have agreed that disciplinary powers shall be exercised in accordance with the Senate's regulations.|مجلس سنا محکمه قضایی و وکلای دادگستری که در سال ۱۹۷۴ تشکیل شده، امروزه می‌تواند به عنوان نهاد حاکمیتی وکلای دادگستری در نظر گرفته شود، چرا که محکمه‌ها از سیاست‌های کلی تعیین شده توسط مجلس سنا پیروی کرده و قضات توافق کرده‌اند که قدرت‌های انضباطی بر اساس مقررات مجلس سنا اعمال شوند.|
|Intending barristers must make a certain number of attendances (known as 'keeping term') at their Inn, and to qualify for Call to the Bar they must either be a law graduate or, if a non-law graduate, have completed the Common Professional Examination.|وکلای دادگستری علاقه‌مند باید تعدادی حضور (شناخته شده به عنوان "حفظ ترم") در محکمه خود داشته باشند، و برای واجد شرایط شدن برای فراخوان به وکلای دادگستری آنها باید یا فارغ‌التحصیل حقوق باشند، یا در صورت عدم فارغ‌التحصیلی از رشته حقوق، امتحان حرفه‌ای عمومی را با موفقیت پشت سر گذاشته باشند.|
|This is followed by a year's Professional Training course before undertaking one year's pupillage in chambers.|این مرحله از یک دوره آموزش حرفه‌ای یک ساله دنبال می‌شود قبل از انجام یک سال کارآموزی در دفتر کار.|
|After six months barristers have the right of audience in any court of law in England and Wales.|پس از شش ماه، وکلای دادگستری حق حضور در هر دادگاه قانونی در انگلیس و ولز را دارند.|
|As noted above, they may take instructions only from a solicitor, not from a lay client direct but unlike the solicitor, they may not sue for their fees.|همانطور که در بالا ذکر شد، آنها فقط می‌توانند دستورالعمل‌ها را از وکیل مشاور دریافت کنند، نه مستقیماً از موکل عادی، اما برخلاف وکیل مشاور، آنها نمی‌توانند برای دریافت هزینه‌های خود شکایت کنند.|

Duties of a Barrister. A barrister intending to practise must choose in which part of the law he or she intends to specialize. A barrister is essentially an advocate whose task is to present his or her client's case effectively in court. Counsel's duty to put their client's case does not extend to advancing the client's unsubstantiated belief that the judge is biased and corrupt. In this case the barrister must either refuse to comply with the client's instructions or withdraw from the case - Thatcher v. Douglas (1996). Their work includes the drafting of opinions on difficult points of law, the settling of pleadings and advice on evidence and procedural matters.
The difference between the two branches of the profession may be summarized as follows:
(a) Barristers are advocates; solicitors are not necessarily so.
(b) Barristers have the right of audience in all courts; solicitors have only a limited right.
(c) Barristers specialize in certain branches of the law; solicitors tend to be general practitioners.
(d) Barristers deal with legal matters; solicitors may be consulted about many non-legal matters, e.g. family, business or financial matters.
(e) Barristers are instructed by solicitors, who are instructed by the lay client.
(f) Barristers cannot sue for their fees; solicitors can.
(g) Barristers may not be liable for negligence in the conduct of a case; solicitors may.
(b) Solicitors are controlled under the Solicitors Acts, 1839–1974; barristers are controlled by their Inns of Court and the recently established Senate, non-statutory bodies.

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|
|Duties of a Barrister.|وظایف یک وکیل دادگستری.|
|A barrister intending to practise must choose in which part of the law he or she intends to specialize.|وکیل دادگستری که قصد دارد تمرین کند باید انتخاب کند که در کدام بخش از قانون تخصص می‌یابد.|
|A barrister is essentially an advocate whose task is to present his or her client's case effectively in court.|یک وکیل دادگستری در اساس یک وکیل مدافع است که وظیفه‌اش ارائه مؤثر پرونده موکلش در دادگاه است.|
|Counsel's duty to put their client's case does not extend to advancing the client's unsubstantiated belief that the judge is biased and corrupt.|وظیفه وکیل به طرح کردن پرونده موکلش محدود نمی‌شود به پیش‌بردن باور بی‌اساس موکلش که قاضی جانب‌دار و فاسد است.|
|In this case the barrister must either refuse to comply with the client's instructions or withdraw from the case - Thatcher v. Douglas (1996).|در این مورد، وکیل دادگستری باید یا از اطاعت دستورات موکل خودداری کند یا از پرونده عقب‌نشینی کند - تاچر در برابر داگلاس (۱۹۹۶).|
|Their work includes the drafting of opinions on difficult points of law, the settling of pleadings and advice on evidence and procedural matters.|کار آنها شامل ترتیب دادن نظرات در مورد نکات دشوار قانون، حل و فصل اظهارنامه‌ها و مشاوره در مورد شواهد و مسائل رویه‌ای است.|

|(c) Barristers specialize in certain branches of the law; solicitors tend to be general practitioners.|(ج) وکلای دادگستری در شاخه‌های خاصی از قانون تخصص می‌یابند؛ وکلای مشاور بیشتر به عنوان پزشکان عمومی عمل می‌کنند.|
|(d) Barristers deal with legal matters; solicitors may be consulted about many non-legal matters, e.g. family, business or financial matters.|(د) وکلای دادگستری با مسائل حقوقی سر و کار دارند؛ وکلای مشاور ممکن است در مورد بسیاری از مسائل غیر‌حقوقی مشورت داده شوند، مانند مسائل خانوادگی، کسب‌وکار یا مالی.|
|(e) Barristers are instructed by solicitors, who are instructed by the lay client.|(ه) وکلای دادگستری توسط وکلای مشاور دستورالعمل می‌یابند، که توسط موکل عادی دستور داده می‌شوند.|
|(f) Barristers cannot sue for their fees; solicitors can.|(و) وکلای دادگستری نمی‌توانند برای دریافت حق‌الزحمه خود شکایت کنند؛ وکلای مشاور می‌توانند.|
|(g) Barristers may not be liable for negligence in the conduct of a case; solicitors may.|(ز) وکلای دادگستری ممکن است در انجام پرونده بابت غفلت مسئول نباشند؛ وکلای مشاور ممکن است باشند.|
|(b) Solicitors are controlled under the Solicitors Acts, 1839–1974; barristers are controlled by their Inns of Court and the recently established Senate, non-statutory bodies.|(ط) وکلای مشاور تحت قوانین وکلای مشاور، ۱۸۳۹-۱۹۷۴ کنترل می‌شوند؛ وکلای دادگستری توسط انجمن‌های قضایی خود و مجلس سنا که اخیراً تاسیس شده‌اند، نهادهای غیر قانونی، کنترل می‌شوند.|

8
Queen's
Counsel
When a barrister has acquired a substantial practice, application may be made to the Lord Chancellor to 'take silk', i.e. become a Queen's Counsel. If the applicant's request is granted, letters patent are issued and he or she will then be called 'within the bar' thus relinquishing their former status of 'outer' or 'utter' barrister. Henceforth they wear a silk gown.
By this new status the successful applicant will expect to attract more important cases and to command higher fees. He or she will no longer draft pleadings, conveyances, or similar documents. Thenceforward they will have the assistance of junior counsel who will be briefed with them. A Queen's Counsel is distinguished by the letters Q.C. after their name, and is referred to as a 'Leader'.

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |

|If the applicant's request is granted, letters patent are issued and he or she will then be called 'within the bar' thus relinquishing their former status of 'outer' or 'utter' barrister.|اگر درخواست متقاضی پذیرفته شود، حکم رسمی صادر می‌شود و او در این وضعیت جدید "در درون میله‌ها" خوانده می‌شود، بدین ترتیب از وضعیت قبلی خود به عنوان وکیل "بیرونی" یا "کامل" دست کشیده است.|

|He or she will no longer draft pleadings, conveyances, or similar documents.|او دیگر اظهارنامه‌ها، انتقالات یا اسناد مشابه را تنظیم نخواهد کرد.|
|Thenceforward they will have the assistance of junior counsel who will be briefed with them.|از آن پس، آنها از کمک وکلای جوان‌تری که با آنها مطلع می‌شوند، بهره‌مند خواهند شد.|
|A Queen's Counsel is distinguished by the letters Q.C. after their name, and is referred to as a 'Leader'.|یک مشاور پادشاه با حروف Q.C. پس از نامشان متمایز می‌شود، و به عنوان یک "رهبر" شناخته می‌شود.|

9 Licensed Conveyancers
A licensed conveyancer is authorized to carry out the legal formalities relat- ing to the transfer of land (Administration of Justice Act, 1985).
The governing body of licensed conveyancers is the Council for Licensed Conveyancers who are responsible for making and enforcing rules on conduct
76 Law Made Simple
and discipline, including accounts rules. The Council is also responsible for the licensing of conveyancers, the first licence being granted on 1 May 1987. Licensed conveyancers are permitted to practise in partnership with each other, or in partnership or association with non-licensed conveyancers (but not with solicitors). In addition to this, licensed conveyancers may practise through the medium of a 'recognized body', which means a body corporate recog- nized by the Council for Licensed Conveyancers.

10
De-regulation
of legal services
up
The Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 has removed many restrictions on the rights of audience in certain county court proceedings. This has opened the right of audience to authorized practitioners and lay representatives. Besides solicitors and licensed conveyancers, conveyancing services are also permitted to be offered by authorized practitioners, who are governed by the Authorized Conveyancing Practitioners Board.11 Legal Services Ombudsman
The Courts and Legal Services Act 1990 also provides for the Lord Chancellor to appoint a Legal Services Ombudsman to investigate allegations into the manner in which any professional legal body has dealt with any complaint made against any of its members.

Outline of the law of persons
All human beings are 'persons' under English law. One of the most import- ant concepts of English law is that all persons within the realm, including aliens, have rights and are subject to certain duties.

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|
|Outline of the law of persons|خلاصه‌ای از قانون اشخاص|
|All human beings are 'persons' under English law.|همه انسان‌ها تحت قانون انگلیس به عنوان 'اشخاص' شناخته می‌شوند.|
|One of the most important concepts of English law is that all persons within the realm, including aliens, have rights and are subject to certain duties.|یکی از مهم‌ترین مفاهیم قانون انگلیس این است که تمام اشخاص در داخل قلمرو، از جمله بیگانگان، دارای حقوق هستند و مشمول وظایف خاصی می‌باشند.|

1
Legal persons
This state of affairs is not universal. Slaves in early Roman and Anglo-Saxon times, for example, had no rights. They were regarded as chattels: a thing to be owned and used or even killed at the will of their master or owner. A slave had, in law, no 'legal personality'. Similarly in early Norman times a criminal could be declared an outlaw: someone outside the law's protection whom any man could kill with impunity. In early times also, animals which had misbe- haved' by attacking humans or cattle were sometimes hanged. In the East gods and idols were offered gifts or appeased in some way as if they were persons. Whether a human being or some other creation is a 'legal person' depends, therefore, on the law of the state where that being or creature is.
In English law legal personality generally attaches to a human being at birth and ends at death. Although certain parts of the criminal law recognize and protect the existence of a child not yet in being', e.g. it is an offence to com- mit abortion or child destruction (which means unlawfully causing the death of a child before it has an existence independent of the mother), this does not necessarily attribute legal personality to the unborn infant.
Kelly v. Kelly (1997)
A father cannot bring an action on behalf of the foetus to restrain abortion because the foetus has no legal personality and no rights which can be enforced before birth.
Death puts an end to both the physical and legal personality. For example, the defamation of a deceased person is not actionable in English law by his personal representatives or near relations.
So far we have been dealing with human beings. We shall later discuss a different kind of legal person, the corporation, which is an artificial or juristic person, created by law, with a legal personality distinct from the individual persons who control the corporation.

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|

|They were regarded as chattels: a thing to be owned and used or even killed at the will of their master or owner.|آنها به عنوان متاع در نظر گرفته می‌شدند: چیزی که می‌توان مالک آن بود و از آن استفاده کرد یا حتی با اراده صاحب یا مالک خود کشته شوند.|
|A slave had, in law, no 'legal personality'.|یک برده، در قانون، هیچ «شخصیت حقوقی» نداشت.|
|Similarly in early Norman times a criminal could be declared an outlaw: someone outside the law's protection whom any man could kill with impunity.|به همین ترتیب در اوایل دوران نورمن یک مجرم می‌توانست به عنوان یک فرد خارج از قانون اعلام شود: کسی که خارج از حمایت قانونی بود و هر کسی می‌توانست بدون مجازات او را بکشد.|

|Kelly v. Kelly (1997)|کلی در برابر کلی (۱۹۹۷)|
|A father cannot bring an action on behalf of the foetus to restrain abortion because the foetus has no legal personality and no rights which can be enforced before birth.|پدر نمی‌تواند به نمایندگی از جنین اقدامی برای جلوگیری از سقط جنین انجام دهد زیرا جنین هیچ شخصیت حقوقی و هیچ حقی که قبل از تولد قابل اجرا باشد، ندارد.|
|Death puts an end to both the physical and legal personality.|مرگ هم به شخصیت فیزیکی و هم به شخصیت حقوقی پایان می‌دهد.|
|For example, the defamation of a deceased person is not actionable in English law by his personal representatives or near relations.|به عنوان مثال، افترا زدن به یک فرد فوت شده از سوی نمایندگان شخصی یا روابط نزدیک او در قانون انگلیس قابل پیگیری نیست.|
|So far we have been dealing with human beings. We shall later discuss a different kind of legal person, the corporation, which is an artificial or juristic person, created by law, with a legal personality distinct from the individual persons who control the corporation.|تا به حال سر و کار ما با انسان‌ها بوده است. بعداً در مورد نوع متفاوتی از شخص حقوقی، شرکت، که یک شخص مصنوعی یا حقوقی است، ایجاد شده توسط قانون، با شخصیت حقوقی متمایز از اشخاص فردی که شرکت را کنترل می‌کنند، بحث خواهیم کرد.|

2 Nationality and
domicile
By a person's nationality we mean their status as a citizen or member of a particular state to which they owe allegiance. Apart from stateless persons everyone is the subject of some state to which they owe political allegiance and loyalty, for which they may be called upon to fight, pay taxes and sup- port, and from which they may expect protection. These are broad general statements only. For instance, although we say that all persons must be
78 Law Made Simple
national subjects of some state or other, we know that due to upheavals of war there are some unfortunate 'stateless' persons who have been disowned by, or expelled from, their country of birth and origin.
Nationality is of great importance in the field of public law. Thus, British subjects enjoy universal franchise, i.e. the right to vote at local and Parliamentary elections. Aliens in Great Britain have no such right. They are subject to certain restrictions concerning entry into the United Kingdom and employment after entry; furthermore they must register certain particulars with the police. Citizens of the European Union, however, have a right to freedom of movement within the State and are not subject to the same restrictions as other aliens.
Apart from these requirements, English law treats aliens in much the same way as ordinary British subjects: for example, they are subject to the same rules of criminal law and the same laws of tort and contract. Special disabil- ities or restrictions will be mentioned later. Here we may note that an alien may not own, or become part-owner of, a British ship registered at a British port and sailing under the British flag.
In the following pages we shall deal with the acquisition of British nation- ality and the allied question of domicile, which is becoming increasingly important as travel makes it easier for people to move from one country to another.

| English Text | Colloquial Farsi Translation |
|--------------|-----------------------------|
|Nationality and domicile|تابعیت و اقامتگاه|
|By a person's nationality we mean their status as a citizen or member of a particular state to which they owe allegiance.|تابعیت فرد به معنای وضعیت او به عنوان یک شهروند یا عضوی از یک کشور خاص است که به آن وفادار است.|
|Apart from stateless persons everyone is the subject of some state to which they owe political allegiance and loyalty, for which they may be called upon to fight, pay taxes and support, and from which they may expect protection.|به جز افراد بی‌وطن، همه افراد متعلق به کشوری هستند که به آن وفاداری و ارادت سیاسی دارند، که ممکن است از آن‌ها خواسته شود برای آن بجنگند، مالیات بدهند و حمایت کنند و از آن انتظار حمایت داشته باشند.|
|Nationality is of great importance in the field of public law.|تابعیت در زمینه قانون عمومی اهمیت زیادی دارد.|
|Thus, British subjects enjoy universal franchise, i.e. the right to vote at local and Parliamentary elections.|بنابراین، اتباع بریتانیا از حق رای عمومی برخوردارند، یعنی حق رای دادن در انتخابات محلی و پارلمانی.|
|Aliens in Great Britain have no such right.|بیگانگان در بریتانیا از چنین حقی برخوردار نیستند.|
|They are subject to certain restrictions concerning entry into the United Kingdom and employment after entry; furthermore they must register certain particulars with the police.|آن‌ها مشمول برخی محدودیت‌ها در مورد ورود به انگلستان و اشتغال پس از ورود هستند؛ همچنین آنها باید برخی جزئیات را نزد پلیس ثبت کنند.|
|Citizens of the European Union, however, have a right to freedom of movement within the State and are not subject to the same restrictions as other aliens.|با این حال، شهروندان اتحادیه اروپا حق آزادی حرکت درون کشور را دارند و مشمول محدودیت‌هایی مانند سایر بیگانگان نیستند.|
|Apart from these requirements, English law treats aliens in much the same way as ordinary British subjects: for example, they are subject to the same rules of criminal law and the same laws of tort and contract.|به جز این الزامات، قانون انگلیس بیگانگان را تقریباً مشابه اتباع عادی بریتانیا رفتار می‌کند: به عنوان مثال، آنها مشمول قوانین یکسان جزایی و همان قوانین دعاوی و قراردادها هستند.|
|Special disabilities or restrictions will be mentioned later.|معلولیت‌ها یا محدودیت‌های خاص بعداً ذکر خواهد شد.|
|Here we may note that an alien may not own, or become part-owner of, a British ship registered at a British port and sailing under the British flag.|اینجا باید ذکر کنیم که یک بیگانه نمی‌تواند مالک یا بخشی از مالکیت یک کشتی بریتانیایی که در بندر بریتانیایی ثبت شده و تحت پرچم بریتانیا حرکت می‌کند، باشد.|
|In the following pages we shall deal with the acquisition of British nationality and the allied question of domicile, which is becoming increasingly important as travel makes it easier for people to move from one country to another.|در صفحات آینده، ما با کسب تابعیت بریتانیایی و سوال مرتبط با اقامتگاه، که با توجه به تسهیل سفر برای افراد جهت جابجایی از یک کشور به کشور دیگر از اهمیت روزافزونی برخوردار است، سروکار خواهیم داشت.|

British nationality
The British Nationality Act, 1981, which replaces the 1948 Act, divides citizenship into three classes:
(i) British citizenship. A person seeking to enter the United Kingdom discharges the burden of proving British citizenship by producing a British passport R v. Secretary of State for the Home Department, ex parte Obi (1997).
-
(ii) British Dependent Territories citizenship, conferred upon citizens of a number of specified countries, e.g. Bermuda. Note that from 1 July 1997 Hong Kong was removed from the list of dependent territories in Schedule 6 of the British Nationality Act, 1981.
The British Overseas Territories Act, 2002, replaced references to Dependant Territory with British Overseas Territory. It also renamed British Dependant Territories citizenship as British Overseas Territories citizenship thereby granting British citizenship to everyone who came within this new classification.
As was stated in Parliament during the passage of the Bill: 'It will mean that every British Dependant Territories citizen will be full British citizens. They will be able to apply for British passports, to live, study, and work in the United Kingdom and the rest of the European Union and share in the benefits of British citizenship that all take for granted.' (iii) British Overseas citizenship - a residual category.
British citizenship may be acquired in the following ways:
(i) By Birth in the United Kingdom to a parent who is a British citizen or who is settled in the United Kingdom, or who becomes a British citizen or becomes settled in the United Kingdom.
(ii) By Adoption under an order made by any court in the United Kingdom authorizing the adoption of a minor who is not a British citizen.

|(i) British citizenship. A person seeking to enter the United Kingdom discharges the burden of proving British citizenship by producing a British passport R v. |(ii) British Dependent Territories citizenship, conferred upon citizens of a number of specified countries, e.g. Bermuda. Note that from 1 July 1997 Hong Kong was removed from the list of dependent territories in Schedule 6 of the British Nationality Act, 1981.|(۲) تابعیت سرزمین‌های تابعه بریتانیا، که به شهروندان تعدادی از کشورهای مشخص شده، به عنوان مثال برمودا، اعطا می‌شود. توجه کنید که از ۱ ژوئیه ۱۹۹۷، هنگ کنگ از فهرست سرزمین‌های تابعه در برنامه ۶ قانون تابعیت بریتانیایی، ۱۹۸۱، حذف شد.|
|The British Overseas Territories Act, 2002, replaced references to Dependant Territory with British Overseas Territory. It also renamed British Dependant Territories citizenship as British Overseas Territories citizenship thereby granting British citizenship to everyone who came within this new classification.|قانون سرزمین‌های برون‌مرزی بریتانیا، ۲۰۰۲، ارجاع‌ها به سرزمین تابعه را با سرزمین برون‌مرزی بریتانیا جایگزین کرد. همچنین نام تابعیت سرزمین‌های تابعه بریتانیا را به تابعیت سرزمین‌های برون‌مرزی بریتانیا تغییر داد، بدین ترتیب تابعیت بریتانیایی را به هرکسی که در این طبقه‌بندی جدید قرار گرفت، اعطا کرد.|
|As was stated in Parliament during the passage of the Bill: 'It will mean that every British Dependant Territories citizen will be full British citizens. They will be able to apply for British passports, to live, study, and work in the United Kingdom and the rest of the European Union and share in the benefits of British citizenship that all take for granted.'|(همانطور که در پارلمان در طی تصویب قانون گفته شد: 'این به این معنا خواهد بود که هر شهروند سرزمین‌های تابعه بریتانیا، تابعیت کامل بریتانیایی خواهد داشت. آنها قادر خواهند بود برای دریافت گذرنامه‌های بریتانیایی اقدام کنند، در بریتانیای کبیر و بقیه اتحادیه اروپا زندگی کنند، تحصیل کنند و کار کنند و در مزایای تابعیت بریتانیایی که همه آن را داده شده فرض می‌کنند، سهیم شوند.')|
|(iii) British Overseas citizenship - a residual category.|(۳) تابعیت برون‌مرزی بریتانیا - یک دسته باقی‌مانده.|
|British citizenship may be acquired in the following ways:|تابعیت بریتانیایی می‌تواند به روش‌های زیر کسب شود:|
|(i) By Birth in the United Kingdom to a parent who is a British citizen or who is settled in the United Kingdom, or who becomes a British citizen or becomes settled in the United Kingdom.|(۱) به واسطه تولد در بریتانیای کبیر از والدی که شهروند بریتانیایی است یا در بریتانیای کبیر اقامت دارد، یا کسی که به شهروندی بریتانیایی درمی‌آید یا در بریتانیای کبیر اقامت می‌کند.|
|(ii) By Adoption under an order made by any court in the United Kingdom authorizing the adoption of a minor who is not a British citizen.|(۲) به واسطه فرزندخواندگی تحت حکمی که توسط هر دادگاهی در بریتانیای کبیر صادر شده که اجازه فرزندخواندگی یک فرد زیر سن قانونی که شهروند بریتانیایی نیست را می‌دهد.|
